meta.author: Mustafa Akben
meta.topic: Planning and hosting a small Saturday-evening dinner party for 6 guests

independentBranchPoints.note: Menu, ambience plan, and drinks plan are independent design tracks once the guest list and dietary needs are fixed; they reconverge at the shopping list builder.

iterativeLoops.exitWhen: All dishes pass salt/acid/seasoning check OR 30 minutes before guest arrival, whichever comes first

nodes.label: Kickoff
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pipeline entry. Triggered the Sunday before the target Saturday. Capture the fixed inputs: date (this Saturday), guest count target (6), location (host's home), and budget ceiling. Hand off to concept.

nodes.label: Set vibe & theme
nodes.purposeInstructions: Concept Agent. Define the evening's vibe in one sentence (e.g., 'relaxed Mediterranean supper, candlelit, conversation-first'). Decide formality (casual / smart-casual), seated vs. buffet, and target end time. Output: a one-paragraph concept brief that all later agents reference. Avoid scope creep — no themes that require costumes or rented equipment.

nodes.label: Build guest list
nodes.purposeInstructions: Guest Curation Agent. Draft a shortlist of 6 guests (plus 2 alternates) who mix well: balance talkers vs. listeners, avoid known interpersonal conflicts, and consider plus-ones already implicitly invited. Output: ordered list with phone/email, dietary flags known in advance, and a 1-line rationale per guest. Do not include anyone the host hasn't seen socially in the last year without flagging it.

nodes.label: Send invites
nodes.purposeInstructions: Invitation Agent. Send a warm, short invite (text or email per guest preference) including: date, arrival time (6:30pm), expected end (~10:30pm), dress code, and a direct ask about dietary restrictions/allergies. Stagger sends so close friends hear first. Track RSVPs in a simple list. Do NOT reveal the guest list to invitees unless asked.

nodes.label: Wait: guest RSVPs
nodes.purposeInstructions: Human-in-the-loop gate. Hold the pipeline until all 6 invitees have responded, OR until Wednesday evening (T-3 days), whichever comes first. The host confirms the final 6 and the consolidated dietary/allergy list before release. If fewer than 6 confirm, host decides whether to invite alternates or proceed smaller — that decision releases this gate.

nodes.label: Plan tracks
nodes.purposeInstructions: Parallel fan-out. With the confirmed guest list and dietary constraints fixed, dispatch three independent planning tracks: menu, ambience, and drinks. They share inputs but produce independent artifacts that reconverge at the shopping list builder. Do not let any track block the others.

nodes.label: Design menu
nodes.purposeInstructions: Menu Agent. Design a 3-course menu (starter, main, dessert) that respects every dietary flag from the RSVPs, leans on seasonal ingredients, and keeps active cooking under 90 minutes the day-of. Favor one make-ahead dish per course. Output: per-dish recipe with serves-6 quantities and a prep-vs-day-of split. Avoid recipes the host has not cooked at least once before.

nodes.label: Plan ambience
nodes.purposeInstructions: Ambience Agent. Plan table setting for 6 (place settings, candles, flowers/centerpiece kept low so people can see across), lighting scheme (dim overheads, lamps + candles), and a 3-hour playlist matched to the concept (background-volume only, no jarring transitions). Output: setup checklist and Spotify/Apple playlist link. Avoid anything fragrant that would compete with the food.

nodes.label: Plan drinks
nodes.purposeInstructions: Beverage Agent. Plan a welcome drink, one wine pairing for the main (or two — one white, one red), and at least two thoughtful non-alcoholic options (not just water/soda). Estimate quantities: ~1.5 drinks/guest welcome, ~half-bottle wine/drinking-guest. Output: drinks list with brands/styles and quantities, flagged by which need chilling time.

nodes.label: Consolidated list
nodes.purposeInstructions: Shopping List Merge. Combine outputs from menu, ambience, and drinks into a single shopping list, grouped by store/section (grocery, butcher/fishmonger, wine shop, florist). Deduplicate items and reconcile quantity overlaps. Output: ordered shopping route that minimizes back-tracking, with a 'do this first' header for items needing pickup-day-of (bread, fish, flowers).

nodes.label: Shop & prep
nodes.purposeInstructions: Procurement Agent. Execute the shopping run on Friday for shelf-stable + non-perishables and Saturday morning for fresh items (fish, bread, herbs, flowers). On Friday evening do all make-ahead prep flagged by the menu (marinades, stocks, dessert that improves resting). Output: kitchen state at Saturday noon — what is already done, what remains. Verify nothing is missing before moving on.

nodes.label: Day-of cooking
nodes.purposeInstructions: Cooking Agent. Starting at T-3 hours (3:30pm for a 6:30pm arrival), execute the day-of cooking schedule: stage mise en place, start the slowest dish first, hold finished components warm, plate sauces last. Keep a single timer/checklist visible. Output: each dish ready within its serving window. Do NOT start anything new in the last 20 minutes before guests arrive.

nodes.label: Taste & adjust
nodes.purposeInstructions: Taste-Test Loop. For each dish nearing completion, taste for salt, acid, fat, heat, and texture. Adjust and re-taste. Continue until the dish passes or until T-30 minutes (then plate as-is — over-tinkering past that point is worse than imperfect seasoning). Track which dishes were adjusted so plating order can compensate.
nodes.loopDefinition: Iterate over each dish; taste, adjust seasoning/acid/fat, re-taste.

stopConditions.text: All 3 dishes pass the salt/acid/seasoning check
Clock reaches T-30 minutes before guest arrival

nodes.label: Host & serve
nodes.purposeInstructions: Hosting Agent. Greet guests at the door, hand off welcome drinks within 2 minutes of arrival, seat by 7:00pm. Serve courses with ~45-minute spacing. Read the room: pause between courses if conversation is good, accelerate if energy is dipping. Refill drinks proactively. Avoid disappearing into the kitchen for more than 5 minutes at a stretch — the host is part of the experience.

nodes.label: Wrap & cleanup
nodes.purposeInstructions: Cleanup & Reflection Agent. After the last guest leaves: load dishwasher, wrap leftovers (label with date), wipe surfaces, take out trash. Next morning: a 5-minute reflection note — what worked, what didn't, which guests should be re-invited together, which dish to repeat or retire. Save the note for the next dinner-party plan. Do NOT attempt deep cleaning the same night.

nodes.label: Done
nodes.purposeInstructions: Pipeline terminus. Dinner served, kitchen reset to baseline, reflection note saved.
